deterministic.style: findings: em dashes in output strings (actions.rs:289,352), comments, README:1959 — house-style, acknowledged (AGENTS permits em dash sparingly)

siblings.targetId: FINDING browser_protocol.json (CDP schema, exempt), inspect_server.rs (direct target_id API, exempt)

radius.note: under-covers: SCIP did not resolve call edges; impacted dominated by tests; treated as orientation only

findings.summary: tab_gone error prints the raw live page URL (token/credential leak); sanitize_url is applied only on the persistence write path, not the runtime-removal error path
findings.evidence: page.url (raw target.url) passed to handle_bound_target_removed at browser.rs:1438,1794 -> bound_target_gone -> tab_gone_error prints ', last url {}' verbatim (browser.rs:392-402); sanitize_url used only at actions.rs:2597. Codex dogfood reproduced token=secret123&user=alice:password in both batch and single-command JSON errors.

findings.summary: tab_switch mutates active index + binding (and the handler clears ref/frame state) before the fallible enable_domains, so a failed switch can strand state
findings.evidence: browser.rs:1388 order; actions.rs:6127 ref/frame clear before switch success. Source-proven; not forced end-to-end.
findings.verified_by: source-read (codex + kai); needs forced enable_domains failure

findings.summary: tab_close removes the page locally and discards Target.closeTarget result, then reports closed:true even if the CDP close failed
findings.evidence: browser.rs:1433,1439 discard close result; response reports closed:true at ~1462. Source-proven; not forced.

findings.summary: Local (non-CDP) launch stores a default-unpinned manager without set_pin_tab (actions.rs:3363,4257); under --pin-tab a locally-launched browser may not enter tab_gone on close
findings.evidence: Contested: writer refuted A end-to-end (round 1); codex re-raises by source read but did NOT dogfood the local-launch path (its dogfood was shared-CDP). pin-tab primary use is shared CDP.
findings.verified_by: source-read (codex); disputed vs writer end-to-end refutation; needs local-launch force

findings.summary: ensure_page (called when page_count==0) creates+attaches a replacement about:blank; codex claims it can bypass tab_gone
findings.evidence: Codex's own cross-session dogfood returned code:tab_gone correctly. The OPEN claim targets the all-pages-gone branch, unforced. Refuted at the dogfooded layer; residual is the page_count==0 branch.
findings.verified_by: dogfood shows tab_gone works (codex); source concern on ensure_page branch unforced

findings.summary: Round-1 partial-path (auto-attach steal) is CLOSED: both Target.attachedToTarget and Target.targetCreated go through register_discovered_page (add_page_without_activation). Guardless test now binds to production.
findings.evidence: actions.rs:960,1091; browser.rs:1845. Codex force-red: flipping the production call to add_page failed test_register_discovered_page_untracked_target_does_not_steal_pinned_tab (active moved AAAA->4F0A); restored green.

findings.summary: Batch propagates pinTab per command and preserves Response::code (tab_gone). daemon_config_fingerprint omits pin_tab, but that is by-design: pin is applied via runtime toggle (actions.rs:2101), proven sticky in dogfood.
findings.evidence: main.rs:1804,1823; connection.rs:591 (fingerprint, intentional). Codex dogfood: --pin-tab against running unpinned daemon -> target pinned:true.

issue_candidates.title: Discarded-first / all-discarded browser can hang connect on the CDP timeout
issue_candidates.evidence: discover_and_attach_targets enables Page.enable on pages[0] (browser.rs:684); Target.getTargets not activity-sorted, no renderer-readiness probe.
issue_candidates.scope: OUT OF SCOPE for #1589 — this is exactly what open PR #1543 fixes (select a live tab at CDP connect). Present on this branch because #1543 not merged.

issue_candidates.title: Pin persistence save/load errors silently ignored on running-daemon toggle
issue_candidates.evidence: actions.rs:2110 discards both binding-load and binding-save Results, so --pin-tab can report success without persisting.
issue_candidates.scope: adjacent to #1589 pin path; small hardening.

issue_candidates.title: Tab-ref parser error text omits the newly accepted target-id domain
issue_candidates.evidence: empty/numeric parse errors still say only 't<N> or a label' (browser.rs:256,280) though resolution accepts target ids.
issue_candidates.scope: in-scope docs/UX nit for #1589's tab <targetId> feature.

exemptions.evidence: CDP DevTools protocol schema field, not the feature's tab-ref resolution.
consumes target_id directly as its WS-proxy API param; does not route through the tab-ref resolver.
AGENTS.md permits em dash sparingly in docs; output-string dashes are house-style, not a gate violation.
